page: Microsoft Azure E32ps_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine instance belonging to the EPSv5 server family. It is powered by an Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor operating at a clock speed of 3.0 GHz. The instance features 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs, utilizing a single thread per core architecture under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is equipped with 208.0 GB of system memory, providing a memory allocation of 6.5 GB per core. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. This instance does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark results place the E32ps_v5 in the average performance tier across multiple workloads. In stress-ng testing, it achieves a single-core score of 1822.48 and a multi-core score of 57228.38. Memory performance is characterized by a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 2231.83 GB/sec, while large block uncached read and write bandwidths reach 171.35 GB/sec and 77.34 GB/sec, respectively. The instance demonstrates solid database capabilities, scoring 21174.02 on PassMark database operations and executing 42.33 million operations per second in Redis SET tests.

While the instance handles lightweight machine learning tasks adequately, processing 489.61 tokens per second for a small 135M LLM model, it exhibits significant performance degradation on larger models. For a 70B LLM model, prompt processing drops to 3.6 tokens per second and text generation to 2.45 tokens per second, which are classified as poor. Consequently, this server is not suited for large-scale AI inference. It is highly appropriate for memory-intensive general-purpose workloads, database operations, static web serving, and software compilation.
description: Microsoft Azure E32ps_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine from the EPSv5 family, running on a 3.0 GHz Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores, 32 vCPUs, and 208.0 GB of system memory, yielding 6.5 GB of RAM per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Under Microsoft Hyper-V, it delivers balanced performance, highlighted by a PassMark database score of 21174.02 and 42.33 million Redis SET operations per second. However, it exhibits a clear performance tradeoff in large-scale machine learning, with poor inference speeds on 70B LLM models. This server is best suited for database hosting, software compilation, static web serving, and general-purpose memory-intensive applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E32ps_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated Ampere Neoverse-N1 cores and 208 GB of RAM, optimized for database operations and general-purpose workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E32ps_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine with 32 dedicated cores and 208 GB RAM, ideal for database and general-purpose workloads.
tagline: An ARM-powered virtual machine offering dedicated physical cores and high memory density for database operations and general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 3.0 GHz Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor with 32 dedicated cores
Equipped with 208.0 GB of system memory and no local storage
Delivers 42.33 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks
Demonstrates poor performance on large-scale 70B LLM inference tasks
Best suited for database hosting, software compilation, and static web serving
